title: Hook-based stateful enforcement for SCOPE→EVIDENCE→VERIFY pipeline
context: Engineers edit code without mandatory verification (tests, typecheck, build), leading to unverified changes shipped. Current approach relies on discipline/checklists.
decision: Implement persistent session state + UserPromptSubmit hook to track product edits, enforce verification commands before next turn, and auto-inject operating loop instructions (soul.md). Domain-classified paths (backend/frontend/tests/infra) map edits to required verify patterns. Unverified edits block or warn on subsequent turns.
consequences: Product code changes are now never processed by next turn until verified (tests/typecheck/build ran)
Operating loop (SCOPE, EVIDENCE, VERIFY) automatically injected at session start and reminded on edit violations
Per-domain verification requirements reduce false verification (don't run frontend tests for backend-only edits)
State persisted across turns allows multi-turn enforcement (edit turn N, verify before turn N+1)
Hook failure mode is silent (exit 0) to avoid blocking the harness on pipeline malfunction
alternatives_rejected: User-facing checklist or guide (not enforced, discipline-dependent)
Post-hoc linting/formatting only (doesn't capture behavior verification)
Synchronous file watching + blocking (coupled to editor, harder to integrate)
Global CLI wrapper (doesn't instrument the harness, no session awareness)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: User-facing checklist or guide (not enforced, discipline-dependent)
Post-hoc linting/formatting only (doesn't capture behavior verification)
Synchronous file watching + blocking (coupled to editor, harder to integrate)
